name: Country Fried Steaks
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, combine 3 tablespoons all-purpose flour, cornmeal, salt, and black pepper. Set aside.
2. Coat the remaining steaks with the combined flour mixture.
3. Beat the egg white and water together. Dip each steak in this batter, then dredge it in the cornmeal mixture.
4. Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook two steaks at a time in 1 tablespoon oil for 5-7 minutes on each side or until they are crisp, lightly browned, and cooked to your liking. Remove the steaks and keep them warm. Repeat with the remaining oil and steaks.
5. While cooking the steaks, prepare the gravy: Melt butter in a saucepan, stir in flour until well blended. Gradually add milk; bring to a boil over medium heat. Boil for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, then reduce the heat to medium-low.
6. Add beef bouillon granules, dried marjoram, dried thyme, and black pepper. Simmer, uncovered, for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
7. Serve the gravy over the steaks.
